one_line_takeaway: PDA is a training-free, black-box, test-time textual defense -- paraphrase the query, decompose into atomic sub-questions, aggregate VLM answers via voting -- that substantially raises adversarial accuracy for VLMs (e.g., VQA-v2 ADV accuracy 0.445 to 0.754 on LLaVA-1.5-7B under PGD ε=2/255) with minimal clean-accuracy cost and no gradient access, though it adds inference latency and its robustness gains shrink (but don't collapse) under adaptive attacks that target its own stochastic pipeline.
key_finding: Under standard white-box PGD (ε=2/255) on LLaVA-1.5-7B/13B (Table 2), PDA raises VQA-v2 adversarial (ADV) accuracy from 0.445 (no defense) to 0.754 (7B) and from 0.593 to 0.787 (13B), and ImageNet-D ADV accuracy from 0.248 to 0.570 (7B) and 0.320 to 0.566 (13B), while also raising clean ImageNet-D accuracy (e.g., 7B: 0.625 clean no-defense to 0.758 clean with PDA). Text-side smoothing baselines (SmoothLLM, SemanticSmooth) gave only modest robustness gains (e.g., SmoothLLM-Swap VQA-v2 ADV 0.453 vs. 0.445 no-defense on 7B) and sometimes hurt clean accuracy. Training-time defenses TeCoA/FARE achieved strong ADV accuracy on some metrics (e.g., FARE VQA-v2 ADV 0.703 on 7B) but at a clear clean-accuracy cost (e.g., FARE clean VQA-v2 0.828 vs. 0.862 no-defense; TeCoA clean 0.787). Under larger PGD budgets (ε up to 8/255) and adaptive EOT attacks that explicitly optimize over PDA's stochastic paraphrase/decomposition sampling, PDA's ADV accuracy degraded somewhat (e.g., ImageNet-D PDA under PGD 0.57 to under EOT-P 0.53) but remained well above the no-defense baseline (0.25 under PGD, 0.20 under EOT-P), indicating the defense does not collapse under attacker awareness. Under black-box transfer/AttackVLM/AnyAttack attacks PDA improved robust accuracy in every case (Table 4, e.g., AttackVLM ImageNet-D 0.56 to 0.61), and under transfer attacks against closed-source APIs (Table 5) PDA improved ImageNet-D/VQA-v2 accuracy for GPT-5 (0.67/0.81 to 0.76/0.90), Claude (0.61/0.82 to 0.70/0.88), and Gemini (0.67/0.76 to 0.72/0.86). PDA also outperformed image-side defenses (JPEG, RA) and was complementary/orthogonal to them (JPEG+PDA and RA+PDA gave further gains), and outperformed reasoning baselines (LLaVA-CoT, self-consistency), which gave much smaller robustness gains since they reason over the same corrupted visual evidence rather than diversifying the query. Ablations show no single budgeted variant dominates across tasks/backbones (Table 7); paraphrase count K saturates quickly (K=3 or 5 typically best; K=7/9 gives no further or even negative gains, Table 8); and stronger LLM agents (GPT, DeepSeek) tend to help decomposition-heavy VQA settings while weaker/local agents (Llama, Qwen) are competitive for verification-heavy ImageNet-D classification (Table 9). Per-sample runtime on ImageNet-D scales with pipeline cost: no-defense 4.5s, PDA-PV 5.5s, PDA-RDA 6.0s, PDA-RJV 10.5s, PDA-Full 15.5s.
core_contribution: The paper's own contribution is PDA (Paraphrase-Decomposition-Aggregation), a training-free, black-box, inference-time defense wrapper for vision-language models (VLMs) against adversarial image perturbations. PDA operates entirely on the textual interface of a frozen VLM: (1) a Paraphrase agent (an LLM) rewrites the user's query into N semantically (or, in one variant, logically) equivalent paraphrases; (2) a Decomposition agent breaks each paraphrase into K atomic, single-fact sub-questions (object presence, attributes, spatial relations, counts, comparisons) answered individually by the victim VLM against the (possibly adversarial) image; (3) an Aggregation agent/voting rule fuses the resulting answers via confidence-/agreement-aware voting (for closed-label tasks) or an LLM summarizer (for open-form tasks like captioning) to produce the final output. The authors motivate this as an 'Expectation over Text Transformations' (EoTT) analogue of randomized smoothing/Expectation-over-Transformations (EOT), instantiated in the text domain rather than the image domain. They also propose four budgeted variants with different LLM-call counts: PDA-Full (full pipeline), PDA-RJV (Reason-Judge-Vote, skips decomposition, LLM judges each paraphrase-level answer), PDA-RDA (Reason-Direct-Aggregate, skips decomposition, single LLM pass over all paraphrase-answer-rationale tuples), and PDA-PV (Paraphrase-Vote, skips decomposition and LLM aggregation, majority vote over VLM answers to paraphrases only).
threat_model: White-box: the attacker has full access to model architecture/parameters/gradients and crafts pixel-level ℓ∞-bounded perturbations (PGD, budget ε; default ε=2/255, swept up to 8/255) to flip the VLM's output on a fixed textual query; the paper also evaluates adaptive attacks (EOT-P, EOT-D, EOT-PD) that explicitly optimize the expected loss over PDA's stochastic paraphrase/decomposition sampling via Monte Carlo approximation, i.e., an attacker aware of and targeting the defense mechanism itself. Black-box: the attacker only has query/transfer access -- evaluated via a transfer-based protocol (surrogate visual encoder, no target gradients), AttackVLM (transfer/query-based targeted attacks), and AnyAttack (large-scale self-supervised transferable attacks), including transfer to closed-source commercial APIs (GPT-5, Claude, Gemini) using adversarial images crafted white-box on LLaVA. The defense itself assumes only black-box (input/output) access to the victim VLM -- it needs no gradients, no retraining, and no architecture-specific hooks, which the authors position against training-time defenses (TeCoA, FARE) that require gradient access and white-box fine-tuning.
limitations: The authors' own text notes: (1) robustness gains shrink under adaptive EOT attacks that explicitly target PDA's stochastic components, though robustness is retained rather than fully broken. (2) No single budgeted variant (PDA-Full/RJV/RDA/PV) is uniformly best -- the optimal choice is task- and backbone-dependent, requiring practitioners to select a variant per deployment. (3) PDA-Full incurs substantial inference overhead (15.5s/sample vs. 4.5s undefended, roughly 3.4x), and even the cheapest variant PDA-PV adds latency (5.5s). (4) Larger paraphrase counts K do not reliably help and can hurt performance (attributed to increased context dispersion and noisier marginal paraphrases inviting non-visual hallucination), so the method requires empirical tuning of K rather than offering a principled selection rule. (5) The paper offers no formal robustness certificate (unlike classical randomized smoothing); it is an empirical defense, and the authors explicitly frame formal certification as future work ('we hope this perspective... will spur research on principled certifications'). (6) The paraphrase/decomposition/aggregation agents are themselves LLMs, introducing dependence on a second model's reliability and potential new attack surface (e.g., prompt injection into the aggregator) that the paper does not evaluate. (7) Results depend on the choice of external LLM agent (Table 9 shows meaningful variance across GPT/DeepSeek/Llama/Qwen), so reported gains are not agent-independent.

quantitative_results: VQA-v2 ADV accuracy (LLaVA-1.5-7B, PGD eps=2/255): No Defense 0.445 to PDA 0.754
VQA-v2 ADV accuracy (LLaVA-1.5-13B, PGD eps=2/255): No Defense 0.593 to PDA 0.787
ImageNet-D Clean/ADV accuracy (LLaVA-1.5-7B): No Defense 0.625/0.248 to PDA 0.758/0.570
COCO CLIPScore ADV (LLaVA-1.5-7B): No Defense 0.753 to PDA 0.774
ImageNet-D ADV accuracy under PGD budget sweep (No Defense to PDA): eps=2/255: 0.25 to 0.57; eps=4/255: 0.22 to 0.56; eps=6/255: 0.19 to 0.53; eps=8/255: 0.17 to 0.54
ImageNet-D/VQA-v2 accuracy under adaptive EOT attacks (PDA): PGD 0.57/0.75, EOT-P 0.53/0.72, EOT-D 0.55/0.70, EOT-PD 0.54/0.74
Black-box attacks (No Defense to PDA), ImageNet-D/VQA-v2: Transfer-based 0.58/0.77 to 0.62/0.81; AttackVLM 0.56/0.75 to 0.61/0.79; AnyAttack 0.61/0.79 to 0.64/0.81
Closed-source API transfer attacks, ImageNet-D/VQA-v2 (No Defense to PDA): GPT-5 0.67/0.81 to 0.76/0.90; Claude 0.61/0.82 to 0.70/0.88; Gemini 0.67/0.76 to 0.72/0.86
Image-side defense comparison, ImageNet-D/VQA-v2 ADV: No Defense 0.25/0.45; JPEG 0.51/0.68; JPEG+PDA 0.61/0.77; RA 0.55/0.70; RA+PDA 0.67/0.80; PDA alone 0.57/0.75
Reasoning baselines, ImageNet-D/VQA-v2 ADV: LLaVA-CoT 0.53/0.51; Self-consistency 0.32/0.49 (vs PDA 0.57/0.75, No Defense 0.25/0.45)
Per-sample inference time on ImageNet-D (LLaVA-1.5-13B victim, RTX A6000): No Defense 4.5s; PDA-PV 5.5s; PDA-RDA 6.0s; PDA-RJV 10.5s; PDA-Full 15.5s
